body: First live calibration run on SLS(68)/WARRANT#54-61 complete (USDJPY M5, 71min, market-close shutdown). Result: honest STAND DOWN, OOS WR 0.0% vs train 33.6%, 3/6 gates (really ~1 real pass -- the other 2 are n=1-sample artifacts), TUNGSTEN's own diagnosis 'signal patterns are time-specific, not structural.' Two big things: (1) WARRANT#56 fired live for the first time and correctly caught/regularized r2 Strong Bear's overfit weights -- direct proof this session's seam fixes solve real problems. (2) Found a NEW bug from the run's own output: GROWTH log showed ' ->  TRILLION in 45 days, WR 65%' one line above a milestone line correctly showing 'WR 0%' -- traced to CalculateRecentWinRate() hardcoding 65% instead of using the real calibrated expectedWinRate (32%), which also feeds a live EV gate, not just display. Fixed as WARRANT#61, matching a pattern already used correctly in 6 other places in the file. Full deep-dive analysis published as an artifact covering what's working, what's broken, the honest gate-by-gate read, and 4 open 'needs reverse engineering' questions (feature thinness, regime granularity, Stage-1's known proxy-scorer gap, and whether the OOS window's calendar placement mattered). Sent: updated source+ex4, the ratchet log, and full session memory.
